channel.title: Carlyle Global Insights
channel.description: Carlyle Global Insights RSS - 自动转换

item.title: A crude awakening
item.description: A disruption in the Strait of Hormuz could affect global energy security, commodity markets, and capital allocation during an incomplete energy transition.

item.title: Software do we go now
item.description: Jason Thomas evaluates the software sector, which has declined 16% since the start of 2026.

item.title: Private markets at an inflection point
item.description: Ruulke Bagijn discusses how investors should assess private equity in the current environment.

item.title: The history of private equity
item.description: David Rubenstein explains how private investment evolved from early private deals to a global industry.

item.title: How I became a fan of Formula 1
item.description: David Rubenstein looks at the rise of Formula 1, exploring how the sport evolved from early car racing into a global platform that blends competition, technology, entertainment, and business.

item.title: You can’t print molecules
item.description: Jeff Currie and James Gutman discuss how Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz is disrupting energy flows, reshaping markets, and accelerating a shift toward energy security.

item.title: Misreading the tea leaves
item.description: Jason Thomas explains how a disorderly unwind in crowded rate trades shows how positioning and hedging flows can drive sharp moves in forward rates and cloud the policy outlook.

item.title: Two clocks, one conflict: Assessing the path forward in Iran
item.description: Adm. James Stavridis, USN (Ret.), assesses the uncertain trajectory of the Iran conflict, outlining key risks and indicators investors can watch to gauge the likelihood of escalation or a near-term ceasefire.

item.title: The risk is time
item.description: Jason Thomas encourages investors to consider that the US labor market entered the current period of energy disruption stronger than many suppose.

item.title: Celebrating Alan Greenspan: Reflections on the maestro
item.description: David Rubenstein reflects on Alan Greenspan’s legacy as he turns 100, offering personal insights on Greenspan’s leadership at the Federal Reserve and his impact on economic policy. #### Pagination * [Page 01](https://www.carlyle.com/global-insights?page=0 "Current page") * [Page 02](https://www.carlyle.com/global-insights?page=1 "Go to page 2") * [Page 03](https://www.carlyle.com/global-insights?page=2 "Go to page 3") * [Page 04](https://www.carlyle.com/global-insights?page=3 "Go to page 4") * ...
